basic.name: Jehun Lee
basic.summary:
KAIST Ph.D. in Industrial & Systems Engineering
SK Hynix · Micron · Samsung Electronics · LG Electronics — 25 projects (PM 12)
SCI journals 4 (1st author 2) · Intl. conferences 6
AI scheduler · AI agent · Simulation-based optimization · Digital twin · Autonomous manufacturing

philosophy.headline: Systemic Excellence
philosophy.lead: Hiring AI agents to pursue systemic excellence — autonomy under human direction.
philosophy.body:
In the AI era, the most important question is not which model to use, but what problem is worth solving — defined through human will and necessity. Algorithms don't choose direction; people do.

My direction is to be a keyman: someone who sees the entire system, maps stakeholders, sets priorities, and deeply understands the problems that need solving so direction can be defined. The growth that lasts is the growth where the organization grows together — not where individuals optimize alone.

Systemic excellence, to me, means hiring AI agents — treating them as employees who execute autonomously, with humans setting direction and confirming key decisions. Chores, basic reviews, missing numbers move into agent pipelines, freeing human attention for the work only humans can do: judgment, strategy, meaning.

pillars.title: Problem Definition
pillars.desc: Mapping stakeholders, setting goals and priorities, defining problems shaped by human will and necessity.

pillars.title: Hiring AI Agents
pillars.desc: Agents execute chores and reviews. Humans focus on judgment, strategy, meaning.

pillars.title: Continuous Inquiry & Application
pillars.desc: Always exploring, always applying. Insight compounds into mastery.

education.institution: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)

thesis.title: Learning Schedulers for Job Shop Scheduling Problems
thesis.topic: Proposes three learning-based schedulers to minimize makespan in job shop scheduling problems (JSSP) where each product has a distinct operation sequence. Designed for flexible selection under varying decision-time constraints, all methods generalize to arbitrary problem sizes regardless of training instances.
thesis.methodology:
Three methods proposed:
1. Learning-based real-time dispatcher: Redesigned state/action/transition using only currently observable information; applied imitation learning instead of RL, achieving superior performance over existing GNN dispatchers.
2. Learning-based local search: Established makespan improvement conditions based on JSSP characteristics (critical blocks, N5 neighborhood), reflected in policy network trained via RL.
3. Depth-limited tree search (SBBS): Combined branch-and-bound with beam search in a lookahead manner, using dispatching strategies as rollout policies for graceful degradation under extreme time constraints.
thesis.performance: SBBS mathematically proven to always produce schedules equal to or better than dispatching-only solutions in static environments. All three methods evaluated on JSSP benchmarks with comparisons to existing approaches; adaptability verified under dynamic event scenarios.
thesis.priorLimitations: GNN-based dispatchers lacked analysis on whether sufficient information was provided for learning optimal strategies. Learning-based local search methods did not fully reflect JSSP-specific characteristics in neighbor selection. Tree-based search could not produce complete solutions under very tight time constraints.

thesis.title: A Genetic Algorithm for Hybrid Flow Shop Scheduling Problems with Multiple Assembly Stages
thesis.topic: Addresses makespan minimization in hybrid flow shop with multiple assembly stages (MA-HFS), where each product consists of multiple parts processed through parallel-machine stages with possible stage-skipping.
thesis.methodology:
Proposed a hybrid genetic algorithm (HGA) with three key contributions:
1. Extended Johnson's algorithm with novel 'operational ready time' and 'operational remaining time' variables for initial solution generation (IGA).
2. Designed crossover and mutation operators reflecting assembly structure.
3. Combined local search on offspring with a lower bound calculation method for MA-HFSSP.
thesis.performance: Achieved superior makespan performance over prior algorithms; outperformed time-limited MIP solver (CPLEX) on large-scale instances.
thesis.priorLimitations: Prior works (Bhongade & Khodke 2014, Nejati et al. 2016) assumed assembly stages only at the end of the layout or ignored parallel machines, limiting applicability. Simple swap-based neighborhood search failed to exploit assembly-stage structure.

workExperience.roles: System designer & PMO: digital twin platform, production-logistics integrated simulator, simulation-based AI scheduler, SaaS product
responsibilities: SK Hynix Digital Twin Platform: production-logistics simulator PM, use-case discovery, KPI definition, data consistency metrics, MES legacy data analysis & simulator integration, AI prediction model data specification
Production-logistics integrated simulation design
Production simulation engine architecture redesign & development participation
Planned and authored 2 government R&D proposals on Agentic AI (manufacturing-specific Agentic AI, LLM-based production plan explanation module / with University of Toronto)
AI extensibility review & design for simulation-based APS, new engine feature definition, participation in GenAI-based development
LLM-based schedule analysis & report generation system performance review and feedback
2 SaaS solution consulting engagements; technical review supporting client executives' strategic decisions (general manufacturing, semiconductor back-end)
WEB UI design participation and feedback

workExperience.roles: Solution consultant: simulation-based real-time APS maintenance, system configuration remote support
responsibilities: Micron global fab simulation-based real-time APS (advanced production and scheduling) system logic maintenance and remote system configuration support

workExperience.roles: Lead researcher: AI-based dynamic schedulers, optimization-based heuristics, autonomous manufacturing scheduling systems
responsibilities: Led full project lifecycle (planning-to-validation): 9 industry-academia projects (planned 5, PM 4), 4 government projects (planned 3, PM 4)
Fundamental research on Neural Combinatorial Optimization based on GNN and RL
Designed general-purpose scheduling agent and Meta-RL methodology for flexible adaptation to manufacturing environment changes

Designed AI-based demand forecasting, production planning, and job scheduling optimization algorithms; validated business impact using real production data (Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, Samsung Heavy Industries, etc.)
Provided technical review supporting client executives' strategic decisions and delivered system design proposals
Introduced structured project management processes (OneDrive & Notion-based) to the lab for the first time
highlights: First Ph.D. graduate of the lab; led project planning, proposal writing, and research beyond typical researcher scope
Won 10-year NRF 'Hanwoomul' long-term project after analyzing rejection feedback and reinforcing proposal logic
2 first-author IEEE journal papers, 7 international conference presentations

workExperience.roles: Scheduling algorithm developer: meta-heuristics, optimization; System designer: digital twin for manufacturing, dynamic rescheduling systems
responsibilities: 5 industry-academia projects (planned 2, PM 2), 4 government projects (planned 2, PM 1)
Led foundational design and operations optimization research for 3D printer-based smart factory digital twin systems
Developed rescheduling algorithms for handling disruptions (machine failures, order cancellations) in flexible manufacturing systems (FMS)
Translated physical manufacturing constraints into mathematical models and validated in virtual environments
Defined virtual factory operation scenarios and conducted simulation-based operational efficiency reviews
Designed and verified real-time scheduling methodologies using genetic algorithms and heuristics
Participated in multiple government projects including FaaS IoT service platform for personalized production; managed deliverables

projects.title: Digital twin platform construction: production-logistics simulation-based operation twin for semiconductor fab

details.situation: SK Hynix required an operation twin for autonomous fab realization, but lacked a unified production-logistics simulation framework to validate process twin consistency with real fab data
details.purpose: Build a production-logistics integrated simulator and define process twin consistency validation framework
tasks: Discovered real operation use-cases for the process twin through stakeholder interviews
Planned and designed production-logistics integrated simulator: unified modeling of process flows and material movement
Integrated real semiconductor fab operational data and performed process twin consistency validation (MES data analysis, SQL)
Defined simulation result consistency metrics and designed/developed automated computation pipeline
WEB UI design participation and feedback
details.achievements: Use-case discovery, KPI definition, consistency metrics definition, automated validation pipeline

projects.title: Software product design/development: simulation-based scheduling for semiconductor manufacturing

details.purpose: Design and develop simulation-based scheduling product for semiconductor manufacturing
tasks: User scenario definition and data schema design

projects.title: Simulation-based real-time scheduler for semiconductor fab

details.situation: Micron's global semiconductor fabs required a next-generation real-time APS scheduler, but existing logic was tightly coupled to specific process constraints, limiting product generality across different fab configurations
details.purpose: Design generalizable real-time scheduler logic by abstracting semiconductor process-specific constraints
tasks: Logic design and review for new real-time scheduler: equipment state changes, priority recalculation, and other core algorithm specification
Abstracted semiconductor process-specific constraints (machine eligibility, batching rules) for improved product generality
Remote solution environment configuration support
details.achievements: Resolved Mozart environment issues; designed logic based on customer requirements

projects.title: Autonomous scheduling for swift and efficient adaptation to dynamic manufacturing environments

details.situation: Existing AI-based schedulers required retraining for each new manufacturing environment. A 10-year NRF initiative was needed to develop autonomous scheduling that adapts to dynamic environments without environment-specific retraining
details.purpose: Develop autonomous scheduling agents adaptable to diverse manufacturing environments; minimize makespan through GNN-based imitation learning for multi-process mixed-flow production
tasks: Led 10-year project planning and execution
Defined progressive extension cases with process constraints and multi-objective functions
Year 1: Developed GNN-based imitation learning methodology for multi-process mixed-flow production that adapts to varying problem sizes
details.achievements: Makespan optimal gap improved by 42.0% (vs. previous SOTA AI); 1 SCI journal paper (1st author)

projects.title: AI-based algorithm for optimal operation plans for various scenarios

details.situation: Samsung Electronics needed accurate demand forecasts for semiconductor products to optimize production planning, but existing methods lacked precision across diverse product categories
details.purpose: Develop AI-based demand forecasting model achieving >80% accuracy across product segments
tasks: Identified decision-making processes through on-site engineer interviews
Defined problem scope and resolution boundaries
Data preprocessing and calibration
Forecasting model conceptualization, discussion, and refinement
Data segmentation

projects.title: Reinforcement learning for unrelated parallel machine scheduling problems with sequence-dependent setup times and machine eligibility
Production planning with AI

details.situation: LG Electronics' refrigerator body assembly line suffered from excessive setup changes and low on-time production rates due to manual planning based on planner experience
details.purpose: Minimize setup count, maximize on-time production rate, and maximize equipment utilization through optimization-based production planning
tasks: Identified on-site constraints and defined problem through engineer interviews (objective: minimize setup count, maximize on-time production and equipment utilization)
Data analysis and calibration (Excel -> Python)
Developed optimal production planning algorithm (optimization engine-based heuristic)
Defined algorithm-based production planning process
details.achievements: 7-day plan with 20min runtime limit: setup reduced 5.1%, on-time production improved 25.8%, idle time reduced 37.6% (vs. on-site manual plan)
details.notes: Achieved better results using optimization engine alone without deep learning

projects.title: Graph-based reinforcement learning algorithm for real-time job shop scheduling
Reinforcement learning for job shop scheduling
Development of a wiring optimization algorithm for X-DEC slim layout
Reinforcement learning-based meta-scheduling for manufacturing systems
Reinforcement learning for project scheduling

details.situation: Samsung Heavy Industries' 6-month shipbuilding schedule suffered from severe daily manpower fluctuations, causing inefficient resource utilization and increased labor costs
details.purpose: Minimize daily worker employment deviation using RL-based schedule adjustment (objective: min std. dev. of daily manpower)
tasks: Led project planning and proposal writing
Defined problem and resolution scope (objective: minimize daily worker employment deviation)
Enhanced ship production simulator
Enhanced RL-based schedule adjustment algorithm
Proposed iterative greedy algorithm
Discussed spatial layout algorithm methodology
details.achievements: Year 1 schedule adjustment: daily manpower std. dev. reduced by 41.60% (RL, 20.27s), 44.53% (greedy, 49.86s), 47.69% (greedy+RL) vs. existing 6-month schedule

projects.title: Development of a reinforcement learning algorithm for workload balancing of ship cargo production
Optimal machine assignment with machine learning algorithms
Cyber-physical assembly and logistics systems in global supply chains

details.situation: In a EUREKA international collaboration (Korea-Sweden), automotive parts assembly required real-time worker allocation optimization to reduce cycle time in semi-automated production lines with varying worker skill levels
details.purpose: Minimize cycle time through real-time worker allocation and conveyor speed optimization in automotive assembly
details.role: Planning, PM, Researcher (global project part lead)
tasks: Identified on-site constraints through field interviews
Defined problem and resolution scope
Developed automotive parts production simulator and real-time worker allocation/reallocation algorithm
Developed conveyor belt speed determination algorithm for semi-automated assembly processes
Defined user scenarios and program screen specifications
details.achievements: Cycle time reduced 5.6% (real on-site data, actual operation method) / 2-5% (vs. meta-heuristic algorithm); Patent registered: KR1020210070359; 1 SCI journal paper (2nd author); 2 conference papers

projects.title: Optimal weight sets for dispatching rules with multiple KPIs
Big data-based simulation and optimization technology for smart manufacturing
Development of scheduling theory and algorithms with reinforcement learning for manufacturing systems
Methodology for dispatching rules' weights
Framework development for KPI analysis with various weights on dispatching rules
Analysis of KPIs according to weights on dispatching rules for LCD manufacturing
Design and analysis for operations optimizations of smart factory testbed
Development of scheduling and rescheduling algorithms for 3D printer-based smart factory
Development of algorithms for detecting and improving inefficient schedules in LCD processes
Development of open FaaS IoT service platform for mass personalization
